terms.definition: MRI sequence where fat appears bright and fluid appears dark, useful for anatomy.
terms.exampleSentence: The T1-weighted images demonstrate the normal anatomy of the lumbar spine clearly.
terms.contextNotes: Fat bright, fluid dark; good for anatomical detail.

terms.definition: MRI sequence where fluid appears bright, highlighting oedema and pathology.
terms.exampleSentence: T2-weighted imaging shows high signal within the meniscus consistent with a tear.
terms.contextNotes: Fluid bright; highlights oedema and most pathology.

terms.definition: Fluid-attenuated inversion recovery MRI sequence that suppresses CSF signal.
terms.exampleSentence: FLAIR sequences highlight periventricular white matter lesions in suspected MS.
terms.contextNotes: CSF suppressed; sensitive for periventricular lesions.

terms.definition: Diffusion-weighted imaging MRI sequence sensitive to acute ischaemia.
terms.exampleSentence: DWI shows restricted diffusion in the right MCA territory consistent with acute infarct.
terms.contextNotes: Pair with ADC map; restricted diffusion = acute stroke.

terms.definition: Paramagnetic MRI contrast agent; contraindicated in severe renal impairment.
terms.exampleSentence: Gadolinium is contraindicated due to her eGFR of 22 and risk of nephrogenic systemic fibrosis.
terms.contextNotes: Avoid in eGFR <30; risk of NSF.

terms.definition: Fear of enclosed spaces, common reason for MRI examination failure.
terms.exampleSentence: The patient declined MRI due to severe claustrophobia and was offered open-bore scanning.
terms.contextNotes: Screen before booking long MRI sequences.

terms.definition: Air in the pleural space causing partial or complete lung collapse.
terms.exampleSentence: The erect chest radiograph demonstrates a large left-sided pneumothorax with mediastinal shift.
terms.contextNotes: Look for absent lung markings and visceral pleural line.

terms.definition: Lung tissue filled with fluid or exudate, typically from pneumonia.
terms.exampleSentence: Right lower zone consolidation is consistent with the clinical diagnosis of pneumonia.
terms.contextNotes: Often with air bronchograms; obscures vessels.

terms.definition: Abnormal fluid collection within the pleural space.
terms.exampleSentence: A moderate right-sided pleural effusion is seen with associated basal atelectasis.
terms.contextNotes: Look for meniscus sign on erect film.

terms.definition: Discrete space-occupying lesion, usually >3 cm in diameter.
terms.exampleSentence: A 4 cm spiculated mass is identified in the right upper lobe, suspicious for malignancy.
terms.contextNotes: Convention: nodule <3 cm, mass ≥3 cm.

terms.definition: Any localised area of abnormal tissue or pathology on imaging.
terms.exampleSentence: Multiple ring-enhancing lesions are seen throughout the cerebral hemispheres.
terms.contextNotes: Generic descriptive term; specify nature when possible.

terms.definition: Bone lesion appearance showing destruction or loss of bone density.
terms.exampleSentence: Multiple lytic lesions in the pelvis raise concern for metastatic disease.
terms.contextNotes: Lucent on radiograph; think myeloma, mets.

terms.definition: Bone lesion appearance showing increased density or hardening.
terms.exampleSentence: A sclerotic lesion in the L3 vertebral body is consistent with prostate metastasis.
terms.contextNotes: Dense on radiograph; classic for prostate, breast mets.

terms.definition: Fracture in which the bone is broken into three or more fragments.
terms.exampleSentence: There is a comminuted fracture of the distal radius with dorsal angulation.
terms.contextNotes: Three or more fragments by definition.

terms.definition: Incomplete paediatric fracture where the bone bends and partially breaks.
terms.exampleSentence: The radiograph shows a greenstick fracture of the mid-shaft radius in this 6-year-old.
terms.contextNotes: Paediatric pattern; one cortex intact.

terms.definition: Fracture caused by torsional forces, with a helical line through the bone.
terms.exampleSentence: A spiral fracture of the humerus in a non-ambulant infant raises safeguarding concerns.
terms.contextNotes: Torsional injury; flag NAI in infants.

terms.definition: Fracture where the broken bone breaches the skin surface.
terms.exampleSentence: The compound fracture of the tibia requires urgent orthopaedic review and antibiotics.
terms.contextNotes: Same as 'open fracture'; high infection risk.

terms.definition: Recent bleeding, appearing hyperdense on non-contrast CT.
terms.exampleSentence: Hyperdense material in the right basal ganglia is consistent with acute haemorrhage.
terms.contextNotes: Hyperdense (~60-80 HU) on non-contrast CT.

terms.definition: Lateral displacement of central brain structures across the midline, indicating mass effect.
terms.exampleSentence: There is 8 mm of midline shift to the right secondary to the left-sided haematoma.
terms.contextNotes: Measure at septum pellucidum; report in mm.

terms.definition: Area of increased density on a radiograph appearing white.
terms.exampleSentence: A focal opacity in the right upper lobe warrants further CT characterisation.
terms.contextNotes: Generic descriptor; specify lobar vs nodular.

terms.definition: Area of decreased density on a radiograph appearing dark or black.
terms.exampleSentence: A well-defined lucency in the femoral neck is suspicious for a lytic lesion.
terms.contextNotes: Opposite of opacity; correlates with lytic on bone.

terms.definition: Hazy increased lung attenuation on CT that does not obscure underlying vessels.
terms.exampleSentence: Bilateral peripheral ground-glass opacities are typical of viral pneumonitis.
terms.contextNotes: Vessels still visible through opacity.

terms.definition: Brain lesion with peripheral contrast enhancement and central non-enhancement.
terms.exampleSentence: A solitary ring-enhancing lesion in the parietal lobe raises concern for abscess or metastasis.
terms.contextNotes: Differentials: abscess, mets, GBM, demyelination.

terms.definition: Crescent-shaped collection of blood between the dura and arachnoid mater.
terms.exampleSentence: A chronic subdural haematoma overlies the left cerebral convexity with mass effect.
terms.contextNotes: Crescent-shaped; crosses sutures, not midline.

terms.definition: Biconvex collection of blood between the skull and dura, often arterial.
terms.exampleSentence: A right temporal extradural haematoma is associated with an overlying skull fracture.
terms.contextNotes: Biconvex (lemon shape); does not cross sutures.
